summary: Large partial decompilation of the Kongo Jungle stage source, adding roughly 1.4k lines to src/melee/gr/grkongo.c plus supporting declarations and type/header adjustments across ground, item, fighter collision, mpLib, and baselib headers. The PR introduced many grKongo_801Dxxxx functions, stage callback wiring, stage parameter data access through grKg_804D6980, Kongo-specific Ground gv fields, randomization helpers, animation/material/platform/item interactions, and function signatures needed by the new code. Review discussion was minimal but noted that broken-function errors after the changes likely meant existing matches were disturbed by a struct layout change or function signature change.
what_changed: Replaced the near-empty grkongo.c with a large partial implementation for Kongo Jungle stage behavior, including initialization functions such as grKongo_801D523C, object creation via grKongo_801D5340, and many per-ground-object setup/update callbacks.
Added grkongo.h declarations and expanded ground type definitions to support Kongo-specific state stored under Ground::gv.kongo/kongo2/kongo3-style fields.

Introduced or exposed supporting external declarations used by grkongo.c, including animation helpers grAnime_801C78FC, grAnime_801C84A4, grAnime_801C7A04, item helper it_802E20D8, and mpLib helpers mpLib_800580AC/mpLib_800580E0/mpLib_800580C8.

Defined a static stage parameter pointer grKg_804D6980 with many currently unnamed fields, populated from Ground_801C49F8(), and used it as the source for timers, rotation bounds, random ranges, speeds, and other Kongo stage parameters.

Added callback-table handling through static StageCallbacks* grKg_803E16E0 and object-spawn logic that calls Ground_801C14D0, configures GX links, stores callback3 into Ground::x1C_callback, and installs callback2 as an HSD_GObj process.

Changed ftColl_80077688 from an untyped void/NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub to a typed signature taking Item*, HitCapsule*, Fighter*, Vec3*, and f32, and added its declaration to ftcoll.h with an it/forward.h include.
Adjusted item, ground, mpLib, groldkongo, and baselib jobj headers to provide types or prototypes needed for the new stage code.

Included several matching-oriented inline helpers and workarounds, such as random_adder, random_adder_f, DegToRad, tau_range, and commented #if 0 scratch/decompiler output blocks for unmatched or partially understood code paths.
smart_moves: Kept the PR explicitly scoped as a partial match rather than pretending the whole grkongo module was understood; many names and fields remain address-based or unk-prefixed where evidence was weak.

Added concrete function signatures only where the new code needed them, such as ftColl_80077688(Item*, HitCapsule*, Fighter*, Vec3*, f32), improving cross-module type information while keeping the function body NOT_IMPLEMENTED.

Used a dedicated static struct pointer for grKg_804D6980 stage parameters rather than scattering raw void* offsets throughout the implementation, making future parameter naming and struct refinement easier.

Preserved decompiler scratch under #if 0 around difficult blocks, providing future contributors with evidence for callback-table layout, OSReport arguments, randomization patterns, and unimplemented item/throw logic without affecting compiled code.
Factored repeated random-range logic into static inline helpers, with a TODO noting that the HSD_Randi-zero-check pattern may recur in other files.

Relied on existing stage/ground infrastructure such as StageCallbacks, Ground_801C14D0, GObj_SetupGXLink, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, grAnime_801C8138, and Ground_801C3FA4 instead of inventing Kongo-specific abstractions prematurely.
decomp_lessons: Large stage files often require simultaneous header/type work across unrelated-looking systems; matching grkongo touched ground, item, fighter collision, mpLib, and baselib declarations.
When a newly introduced struct layout or function signature causes 'broken functions' in CI, existing matches may no longer match even if their source files were not directly worked on.

For stage parameter blobs, an anonymous struct with exact offset-like unk fields can be a useful interim step; it makes access patterns compilable and searchable while leaving semantic names for later passes.
Do not over-name fields from behavior guesses. This PR mostly retained unk0/unk4/unk34/etc. and gv.kongo offset-style fields, which is safer for a partial match with limited semantic confidence.

Callback table indexing is sensitive: grKongo_801D5340 used &grKg_803E16E0[gobj_id + 1], then callback0/callback2/callback3 in specific roles. Future stage work should verify callback-table base and element layout carefully.
Random integer range generation around HSD_Randi needs exact zero-difference behavior and cast ordering for matching; helper simplification may fail if it changes codegen.
Float constants and conversions such as DegToRad, M_TAU, f32/f64 casts, and tau wrapping are important matching levers in stage motion code.
Partially matched switch/state-machine code may preserve odd constructs, fallthrough-like cases, or commented-out blocks until the exact control flow is recovered.
naming_conventions: Stage functions follow the address-based module prefix pattern grKongo_801Dxxxx.
Static helper functions in the file use fn_801Dxxxx where no semantic name is known.
Stage-specific globals use the grKg_ prefix, including grKg_803E16E0 for StageCallbacks and grKg_804D6980 for parameter data.
Unknown struct fields remain offset-like names such as unk0, unk4, unk34, unk84 or xC4/xC6/xD8/xE0 under Ground::gv.kongo variants.
Existing project capitalization is preserved for known systems: Ground_801Cxxxx, Stage_80225194, ftColl_80077688, mpLib_80057BC0, HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj.
Header additions used forward declarations where possible, e.g. ftcoll.h added it/forward.h instead of including full item definitions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in several setup functions to satisfy stack layout/codegen requirements.
Kept #if 0 decompiler pseudocode beside rewritten source for difficult functions, especially callback setup and later item/throw logic.
Added inline helpers for repeated random range patterns, but retained explicit cast-heavy versions where simpler helpers likely did not match.
Preserved exact f32 constants such as 0.017453292F and double constant M_TAU 6.283185307179586 for angle conversion and wrapping behavior.
Used explicit f32/f64 casts in tau_range and rotation logic, suggesting codegen sensitivity around floating-point comparison and subtraction.
Left suspicious or unresolved control-flow comments such as 'weird', disabled goto/block labels, and commented calls where the matching or type evidence was incomplete.
Typed ftColl_80077688 parameters to satisfy call sites while leaving implementation as NOT_IMPLEMENTED, a common tactic when a dependency signature is needed before the callee is decompiled.

review_feedback.author: thefoxcam
review_feedback.comment: Not sure what's causing the broken functions error; all but grKongo_801D828C haven't had any prior work done on them.
review_feedback.lesson: A PR can break previously matched functions indirectly even when most newly touched functions had no prior work.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: It means they no longer match. Probably a struct change somewhere.
review_feedback.lesson: Broken-function errors after type work often point to struct layout changes affecting existing codegen.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Could also be a function signature change.
review_feedback.lesson: Function prototype changes can also disturb matches, so investigate both struct layouts and signatures when CI reports broken functions.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs that continue grkongo matching, especially functions around grKongo_801D8078, grKongo_801D8134, grKongo_801D828C, and grKongo_801D8314.
Search for later renames or struct refinements of grKg_804D6980 fields unk0 through unk98.
Search for later changes to Ground::gv.kongo, gv.kongo2, and gv.kongo3 layouts caused by this PR or follow-up fixes.
Investigate whether ftColl_80077688 signature remained Item*, HitCapsule*, Fighter*, Vec3*, f32 or was revised later.
Look for follow-up fixes addressing the broken-function error mentioned in comments, especially struct layout or function signature corrections.
Compare random_adder/HSD_Randi-zero-check patterns in other stage files to see whether this TODO was generalized.
